column67:
AUTO1 Group is a technology company based in Germany, founded in 2012 by Christian Bertermann and Hakan Koç. It focuses on enhancing Europe's used car market through digital platforms that facilitate the buying, selling, and financing of vehicles. Operating in over 30 countries, AUTO1 Group is recognized as Europe's leading digital automotive platform, offering haggle-free pricing and a vast selection of used cars.

The company has experienced significant growth, launching AUTO1.com in 2013 as a wholesale platform and going public in February 2021. By the end of 2024, AUTO1 Group had sold 689,700 cars and traded approximately 5.5 million vehicles. It employs around 6,300 people and supports over 60,000 active dealers. AUTO1 Group provides integrated solutions throughout the used car lifecycle, including consumer selling brands, a wholesale platform, retail buying options through Autohero, and fully digital financing solutions.

column70:
IAV GmbH is a global engineering company based in Berlin, Germany, specializing in automotive and mobility technology development. Founded in 1983 as a university-affiliated research institute, IAV has grown to employ over 8,000 staff across multiple development centers in Germany and various international locations, including France, the UK, and China. The company focuses on innovation in areas such as artificial intelligence, software-defined vehicles, autonomous driving, and electric vehicles, with an estimated annual revenue of around €910.6 million.

IAV offers a wide range of engineering services throughout the vehicle lifecycle, including powertrain systems development, electronics and software systems, and vehicle integration. The company also develops advanced products like e-mobility control units and software-defined vehicle platforms, supporting the transition to electric vehicles and future mobility needs. IAV serves major automobile manufacturers and component suppliers, contributing to the advancement of sustainable technologies in various sectors.

column71:
DATEV eG is a cooperative based in Nuremberg and ranks as one of Europe's largest software companies, specifically the third-largest provider of business software in Germany. With over 8,900 employees, DATEV supports around 620,000 customers, including small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), tax consultants, and various institutions. The company focuses on digital collaboration for business management, emphasizing information security, data protection, and tax compliance.

DATEV offers more than 200 software products and IT services designed to digitalize commercial processes. Their key offerings include business software for accounting and financial management, cloud solutions, e-invoicing tools, and a developer ecosystem for data exchange. The company also provides training and consulting services to help professionals transition to DATEV systems. Committed to sustainability and good corporate governance, DATEV actively shapes digital progress for its community of 2.8 million businesses and professionals.

column67:
AUTO1 Group is a technology company based in Germany, founded in 2012 by Christian Bertermann and Hakan Koç. It focuses on enhancing Europe's used car market through digital platforms that facilitate the buying, selling, and financing of vehicles. Operating in over 30 countries, AUTO1 Group is recognized as Europe's leading digital automotive platform, offering haggle-free pricing and a vast selection of used cars.

The company has experienced significant growth, launching AUTO1.com in 2013 as a wholesale platform and going public in February 2021. By the end of 2024, AUTO1 Group had sold 689,700 cars and traded approximately 5.5 million vehicles. It employs around 6,300 people and supports over 60,000 active dealers. AUTO1 Group provides integrated solutions throughout the used car lifecycle, including consumer selling brands, a wholesale platform, retail buying options through Autohero, and fully digital financing solutions.

column70:
IAV GmbH is a global engineering company based in Berlin, Germany, specializing in automotive and mobility technology development. Founded in 1983 as a university-affiliated research institute, IAV has grown to employ over 8,000 staff across multiple development centers in Germany and various international locations, including France, the UK, and China. The company focuses on innovation in areas such as artificial intelligence, software-defined vehicles, autonomous driving, and electric vehicles, with an estimated annual revenue of around €910.6 million.

IAV offers a wide range of engineering services throughout the vehicle lifecycle, including powertrain systems development, electronics and software systems, and vehicle integration. The company also develops advanced products like e-mobility control units and software-defined vehicle platforms, supporting the transition to electric vehicles and future mobility needs. IAV serves major automobile manufacturers and component suppliers, contributing to the advancement of sustainable technologies in various sectors.

column71:
DATEV eG is a cooperative based in Nuremberg and ranks as one of Europe's largest software companies, specifically the third-largest provider of business software in Germany. With over 8,900 employees, DATEV supports around 620,000 customers, including small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), tax consultants, and various institutions. The company focuses on digital collaboration for business management, emphasizing information security, data protection, and tax compliance.

DATEV offers more than 200 software products and IT services designed to digitalize commercial processes. Their key offerings include business software for accounting and financial management, cloud solutions, e-invoicing tools, and a developer ecosystem for data exchange. The company also provides training and consulting services to help professionals transition to DATEV systems. Committed to sustainability and good corporate governance, DATEV actively shapes digital progress for its community of 2.8 million businesses and professionals.

column67:
AUTO1 Group is a technology company based in Germany, founded in 2012 by Christian Bertermann and Hakan Koç. It focuses on enhancing Europe's used car market through digital platforms that facilitate the buying, selling, and financing of vehicles. Operating in over 30 countries, AUTO1 Group is recognized as Europe's leading digital automotive platform, offering haggle-free pricing and a vast selection of used cars.

The company has experienced significant growth, launching AUTO1.com in 2013 as a wholesale platform and going public in February 2021. By the end of 2024, AUTO1 Group had sold 689,700 cars and traded approximately 5.5 million vehicles. It employs around 6,300 people and supports over 60,000 active dealers. AUTO1 Group provides integrated solutions throughout the used car lifecycle, including consumer selling brands, a wholesale platform, retail buying options through Autohero, and fully digital financing solutions.

column70:
IAV GmbH is a global engineering company based in Berlin, Germany, specializing in automotive and mobility technology development. Founded in 1983 as a university-affiliated research institute, IAV has grown to employ over 8,000 staff across multiple development centers in Germany and various international locations, including France, the UK, and China. The company focuses on innovation in areas such as artificial intelligence, software-defined vehicles, autonomous driving, and electric vehicles, with an estimated annual revenue of around €910.6 million.

IAV offers a wide range of engineering services throughout the vehicle lifecycle, including powertrain systems development, electronics and software systems, and vehicle integration. The company also develops advanced products like e-mobility control units and software-defined vehicle platforms, supporting the transition to electric vehicles and future mobility needs. IAV serves major automobile manufacturers and component suppliers, contributing to the advancement of sustainable technologies in various sectors.

column71:
DATEV eG is a cooperative based in Nuremberg and ranks as one of Europe's largest software companies, specifically the third-largest provider of business software in Germany. With over 8,900 employees, DATEV supports around 620,000 customers, including small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), tax consultants, and various institutions. The company focuses on digital collaboration for business management, emphasizing information security, data protection, and tax compliance.

DATEV offers more than 200 software products and IT services designed to digitalize commercial processes. Their key offerings include business software for accounting and financial management, cloud solutions, e-invoicing tools, and a developer ecosystem for data exchange. The company also provides training and consulting services to help professionals transition to DATEV systems. Committed to sustainability and good corporate governance, DATEV actively shapes digital progress for its community of 2.8 million businesses and professionals.

column67:
Atruvia AG is a digitalization partner for the German cooperative financial group, offering specialized IT solutions and services tailored for banks. With decades of expertise in banking and information technology, the company provides a range of offerings, including data center operations, proprietary banking software, and app development. Atruvia serves 866 banks, processes over 85 million accounts, and handles more than 7.8 billion transactions annually.

The company emphasizes process optimization and regulatory compliance while leveraging advanced technologies like Smart Data. Atruvia aims to enhance market relevance and ensure sustainable profitability, positioning itself as an innovative leader in the banking sector. Its commitment to connectivity and customer-centric approaches is reflected in its slogan "einfach. sicher. digital." (simple. secure. digital.). Additionally, Atruvia promotes career opportunities through training programs, internships, and entry-level positions.

column70:
CANCOM SE is a prominent IT services provider based in Munich, Germany, established in 1992. The company specializes in delivering a wide range of digital business solutions, including hybrid IT services, cloud services, managed services, and consulting. With over 5,600 employees and around 80 locations across Germany, Austria, Belgium, Slovakia, Romania, and the Czech Republic, CANCOM supports organizations in their digital transformation efforts, primarily in the DACH region and internationally.

The company offers a comprehensive IT portfolio that includes supply services, managed services, professional services, and support services. Key focus areas include cloud services, AI expansion, and simplifying IT complexity for new business models. CANCOM serves various sectors such as retail, manufacturing, healthcare, education, and public administration, contributing to its revenue of approximately €1.7 billion in 2024. The company is listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange and emphasizes sustainability and digital resilience in its operations.

column61: Vistaprint USA Incorporated, 170 Data Drive, Waltham, MA 02451, US
column68:
Vista is a design and marketing partner for millions of small businesses around the world. Founded in 1995 as VistaPrint, the company has evolved from a direct mail catalog to a leading online platform for customizable marketing solutions. As a Cimpress company, Vista operates globally with a remote-first team across more than 25 countries.

Vista offers a wide range of services, including business cards, marketing materials, signage, promotional products, and digital marketing services. The company provides user-friendly design tools and templates, making it accessible for users without professional design experience. Customers can also benefit from real-time support and expert assistance. Additionally, VistaPrint Corporate Solutions caters to larger businesses with tailored services, including personalized storefronts and professional design support.

column62: EDAG Engineering Group AG, Kreuzberger Ring 40, Wiesbaden, Hessen 65205, DE
column69:
EDAG Group is a global engineering service provider that specializes in integrated automotive and industrial solutions. Founded in 1969, the company has grown to employ approximately 9,000 people and operates across various industries worldwide. EDAG has established itself as one of the largest independent engineering service providers in the automotive sector.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire development chain, from initial concept to production-ready solutions. Key areas of expertise include vehicle development, smart factory and production engineering, software development, and manufacturing optimization. EDAG's integrated engineering approach combines these services to deliver efficient and innovative solutions. They serve diverse sectors, including automotive, defense, medical, energy, and industrial applications, and have specialized brands like EDAG Production Solutions and EDAG Aeromotive to cater to specific market needs.

column67:
Dataport AöR is Germany's leading IT service provider for public administrations, focusing on digitalization, IT infrastructure, and comprehensive services tailored exclusively for the public sector. Established on January 1, 2004, through a merger of various regional data centers and IT offices, Dataport is headquartered in Altenholz near Kiel and operates branches in several cities, including Hamburg, Rostock, and Bremen.

As a not-for-profit entity owned by six German federal states, Dataport employs around 5,500 people and is projected to generate €1.42 billion in revenue in 2024. The company offers end-to-end IT solutions, including infrastructure operations, specialized software development, IT procurement, training, and project management. It emphasizes digital sovereignty, IT security, and innovation to support the digital transition of governmental services. Dataport serves state and local governments, focusing on enhancing administrative procedures for authorities and citizens.

column69:
EDAG Group is a global engineering service provider that specializes in integrated automotive and industrial solutions. Founded in 1969, the company has grown to employ approximately 9,000 people and operates across various industries worldwide. EDAG has established itself as one of the largest independent engineering service providers in the automotive sector.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire development chain, from initial concept to production-ready solutions. Key areas of expertise include vehicle development, smart factory and production engineering, software development, and manufacturing optimization. EDAG's integrated engineering approach combines these services to deliver efficient and innovative solutions. They serve diverse sectors, including automotive, defense, medical, energy, and industrial applications, and have specialized brands like EDAG Production Solutions and EDAG Aeromotive to cater to specific market needs.

column63:
Schwarz Digits is the IT and digital division of the Schwarz Group, a prominent German retail conglomerate known for its brands like Lidl and Kaufland. Headquartered in Neckarsulm, Germany, Schwarz Digits focuses on providing GDPR-compliant digital products and services that prioritize data protection and digital independence. The company aims to empower European organizations to manage their digital strategies autonomously, aligning with European values.

The division offers a range of digital solutions, including the STACKIT cloud infrastructure, which is an open-source-based platform expanding with new data centers. It also provides cybersecurity solutions through XM Cyber, IT infrastructure, software development, and consulting services. With around 8,000 employees, Schwarz Digits supports both internal companies within the Schwarz Group and external clients, including FC Bayern Munich, by delivering scalable solutions for secure data management and IT protection. The company is committed to enhancing resilience and security in the digital landscape, making it a key player in cloud and AI technologies in Europe.

column67:
DB Systel GmbH is the IT subsidiary of Deutsche Bahn AG, based in Frankfurt am Main, Germany. Founded in 2007, the company employs around 5,400 people and operates in 13 locations across Germany. With a revenue of approximately $1.3 billion, DB Systel is a vital partner in the digital transformation of rail transport operations, infrastructure, and customer services within the DB Group.

The company specializes in a range of digital services and technology solutions, including digital consulting, cloud computing, cybersecurity, data management, and software development. DB Systel focuses on enhancing efficiency and safety in the rail sector through innovative technologies such as artificial intelligence, big data, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Its projects often involve collaboration across the organization, aiming to improve the overall customer experience and operational effectiveness for internal DB Group companies.

column68:
CompuGroup Medical SE & Co. KGaA, also known as CGM, is a German software company based in Koblenz. It specializes in developing software solutions for the healthcare sector and operates in over 60 countries with a workforce of more than 8,700 employees. CGM is recognized as a leading provider of healthcare technology.

The company offers a wide range of products, including Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems, practice management software, hospital information systems, and telehealth solutions. Additionally, CGM provides laboratory information systems, revenue cycle management services, and pharmacy information systems. Their innovative solutions support various healthcare organizations, such as doctors' practices, hospitals, pharmacies, and health insurance companies.

In 2024, CGM reported significant revenue, particularly from its ambulatory information systems and hospital information systems. The company serves a diverse clientele across multiple regions, including Europe, Asia, and the United States.

column69:
Shopify is a global commerce platform founded in 2006 in Ottawa, Canada, by Tobias Lütke, Daniel Weinand, and Scott Lake. It enables millions of merchants in around 175 countries to build, manage, and scale their online and in-person businesses. Originally created as a custom e-commerce solution for a snowboarding store, Shopify has evolved into a comprehensive ecosystem that supports a wide range of commerce needs.

The platform offers a complete suite of tools, including customizable online stores, integrated payment processing through Shopify Payments, and a Point of Sale (POS) system for in-person retail. With over 8,000 apps available in its App Store and more than 900 themes in its Theme Store, Shopify provides extensive functionality for merchants. The company emphasizes accessibility, scalability, and innovation, making it a preferred choice for businesses of all sizes, from small retailers to large brands. As of late 2024, Shopify continues to be a dominant player in digital retail, processing significant sales globally.

column70:
CANCOM SE is a prominent IT services provider based in Munich, Germany, established in 1992. The company specializes in delivering a wide range of digital business solutions, including hybrid IT services, cloud services, managed services, and consulting. With over 5,600 employees and around 80 locations across Germany, Austria, Belgium, Slovakia, Romania, and the Czech Republic, CANCOM supports organizations in their digital transformation efforts, primarily in the DACH region and internationally.

The company offers a comprehensive IT portfolio that includes supply services, managed services, professional services, and support services. Key focus areas include cloud services, AI expansion, and simplifying IT complexity for new business models. CANCOM serves various sectors such as retail, manufacturing, healthcare, education, and public administration, contributing to its revenue of approximately €1.7 billion in 2024. The company is listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange and emphasizes sustainability and digital resilience in its operations.

column69:
Shopify is a global commerce platform founded in 2006 in Ottawa, Canada, by Tobias Lütke, Daniel Weinand, and Scott Lake. It enables millions of merchants in around 175 countries to build, manage, and scale their online and in-person businesses. Originally created as a custom e-commerce solution for a snowboarding store, Shopify has evolved into a comprehensive ecosystem that supports a wide range of commerce needs.

The platform offers a complete suite of tools, including customizable online stores, integrated payment processing through Shopify Payments, and a Point of Sale (POS) system for in-person retail. With over 8,000 apps available in its App Store and more than 900 themes in its Theme Store, Shopify provides extensive functionality for merchants. The company emphasizes accessibility, scalability, and innovation, making it a preferred choice for businesses of all sizes, from small retailers to large brands. As of late 2024, Shopify continues to be a dominant player in digital retail, processing significant sales globally.

column71:
DATEV eG is a cooperative based in Nuremberg and ranks as one of Europe's largest software companies, specifically the third-largest provider of business software in Germany. With over 8,900 employees, DATEV supports around 620,000 customers, including small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), tax consultants, and various institutions. The company focuses on digital collaboration for business management, emphasizing information security, data protection, and tax compliance.

DATEV offers more than 200 software products and IT services designed to digitalize commercial processes. Their key offerings include business software for accounting and financial management, cloud solutions, e-invoicing tools, and a developer ecosystem for data exchange. The company also provides training and consulting services to help professionals transition to DATEV systems. Committed to sustainability and good corporate governance, DATEV actively shapes digital progress for its community of 2.8 million businesses and professionals.

column67:
BWI GmbH is a German IT system house and a fully federally owned limited liability company based in Meckenheim, Germany. With around 7,700 employees, it serves as the primary digitalization partner for the Bundeswehr (German Armed Forces) and other federal entities. The company focuses on providing stable, secure, and efficient IT operations, enhancing the Bundeswehr's command and operational capabilities through a range of hardware, system environments, and services.

BWI offers a variety of IT services, including IT infrastructure and operations, system management and support, and digitalization initiatives. Its expertise spans cloud technology, AI, and secure communications, supporting key projects like health care digitalization and data protection. BWI emphasizes innovation, diversity, and compliance with data protection regulations, and it is committed to career development through the BWI Academy. The company has received recognition for its performance, including three Stevie Awards in 2016.

column73:
Springer Nature is a leading global academic publishing company formed in May 2015 through the merger of several established brands, including Springer Science+Business Media and Nature Publishing Group. With a rich history dating back to 1842, it has grown to employ over 9,000 staff across 200 offices in 50 countries. The company has published more than 240,000 books and offers a vast portfolio of over 3,000 journals, including the renowned *Nature*.

Specializing in scientific, technical, medical (STM), and educational content, Springer Nature is the largest open access publisher, providing a wide range of journals, articles, and books. It publishes approximately 11,000 new titles each year and has digitized its extensive backlist. The company also offers digital platforms and services to enhance discoverability and support authors and editors. With a commitment to advancing science and education, Springer Nature collaborates with a global network of academic professionals and institutions.
